sections.paragraph:
αἱματεκχυσία, ας, ἡ (fr. αἷμα and ἐκχέω, s. αἷμα 2a) the shedding or pouring out of blood (found only in Christian wr., e.g. Tat. 23, 2; Byz. Chroniclers in Psaltes p. 349, but cp. ἔκχυσις αἵματος 3 Km 18:28; Sir 27:15; Charax of Pergamum [II/III a.d.]: 103 Fgm. 5 Jac.) χωρὶς αἱ. οὐ γίνεται ἄφεσις without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness Hb 9:22 (blood offered at the altar is the referent; s. TThornton, JTS n.s. 15, ’64, 63–65, w. ref. to such passages as Lev 4:7, 18, 25 al.)—TW.

  1. fr. = from
  2. wr. = writers, writings

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Charax of Pergamum Charax of Pergamum , II? a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

ref. ref. = reference(s)

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 27
